Definition: A globally unique ID for every speedtest performed based.
Denotes the platform on which the test was taken:1 = Android2 = iOS3 = Web4 = MacOS5 = Windows
The ID number assigned to a server
The name of the server used for the test. This is typically the city where the server is located.
The name of the test server sponsor. Combining server name and server sponsor will create a unique name for each server.
The latitude of the server used for the test.
The longitude of the server used for the test.
The country where the server used for the test is located.
The two letter country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of where the test server is located.
The minimum latency value recorded during server selection, measured in milliseconds.
The minimum jitter value for the recorded during server selection, measured in milliseconds.
A comma-separated list of latency pings performed during server selection, measured in milliseconds.
TRUE if the server is on network.
Indicates if the individual latency measurement was included in the multi-server latency calculation.
